home.hero_subtitle: Learn all 23 Gang of Four design patterns with clear examples, diagrams, and real-world code.
home.category_creational_desc: Object creation mechanisms for flexible, reusable code.
home.category_structural_desc: Compose objects into larger, flexible structures.
home.category_behavioral_desc: Efficient communication and responsibility between objects.
home.section_featured_sub: The most widely used GoF patterns
home.oss_desc: Design Patterns Academy is built in the open. Contributions, feedback, and pull requests are welcome.

catalog.subtitle: Choose a learning path. More modules arriving soon.

about.lead: A premium open-source educational platform for learning the 23 Gang of Four design patterns — built with modern web standards, no frameworks, and a passion for great developer experience.
about.project_p1: Design Patterns Academy was created to provide a clean, distraction-free learning experience for one of the most important topics in software engineering: design patterns.
about.project_p2: Unlike many resources, every pattern explanation here comes with clear intent, real-world context, implementation examples in multiple languages, and honest trade-off analysis.
about.project_p3: The platform is designed to grow into a full knowledge catalog covering algorithms, data structures, software architecture, and more — all following the same editorial standards.
about.philosophy_p1: Every decision — from typography to component structure — follows the same principles that the patterns themselves teach: separation of concerns, single responsibility, and clean interfaces.
about.tooltip_solid: Five object-oriented design principles that help create maintainable, flexible and scalable software.
about.tooltip_dry: Don't Repeat Yourself. Avoid duplicating knowledge or business logic in multiple places.
about.tooltip_kiss: Keep It Simple, Stupid. Prefer the simplest solution that correctly solves the problem.
about.tooltip_yagni: You Aren't Gonna Need It. Do not implement functionality until it is actually required.
about.tooltip_semantic_html: Use HTML elements according to their meaning to improve accessibility and SEO.
about.tooltip_accessibility: Design interfaces that are usable by everyone, including people with disabilities.
about.tooltip_performance: Performance is a product requirement, not an afterthought.
about.tooltip_mobile_first: Design for mobile devices first, then progressively enhance larger screens.
about.stack_intro: Zero frameworks. Every feature is intentionally built with the platform stack it demonstrates.
about.tech_why_css: Design tokens, themes, zero runtime cost
about.tech_why_vite: Instant HMR, optimised builds, code splitting
about.roadmap_intro: The current version covers all 23 Gang of Four Design Patterns. Future modules planned:
about.contribute_desc: Found an error? Want to add a language example? Have a pattern suggestion? All contributions are welcome.
